title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
prompt: Given the head of a linked list, reverse the nodes of the list k at a time, and return the modified list. Nodes left over at the end (fewer than k) remain in their original order. Use only O(1) extra memory.
hints: Count the length first to know how many full groups exist.
For each full group, reverse k nodes and stitch the boundaries with the previous group's tail.
pitfalls: Losing the next group's pointer mid-reverse.
